title: The basement of this $4.7 m Lynnfield home known for its holiday decor is like a private club
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: 1 Haywood Farm in Lynnfield, owned by Matthew Maggiore, is listed for $4.695 million.
The home's basement features a bar, screening room, kids' playroom, wine tasting room, and sauna.
The property is known for elaborate holiday decor, attracting thousands of visitors during Halloween.

title: Patriots reportedly set to release QB Joshua Dobbs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Patriots are reportedly planning to release quarterback Joshua Dobbs, according to NFL Network's Ian Rapoport.
Dobbs, 31, appeared in four games last season, completing seven of ten passes for 65 yards as a backup.
New England recently re-signed quarterback Tommy DeVito, which may have influenced the decision to release Dobbs.

title: Tracy McGrady questions whether Celtics are ‘still on same the page’ following Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tracy McGrady questions Celtics' cohesion after Jayson Tatum's return from a 10-month Achilles rehab.
Celtics' shooting struggles noted, with a 36% field goal percentage against Minnesota and 45% against Memphis.
Carmelo Anthony suggests Tatum focus more on inside play, as 53% of his shots have been 3-pointers this season.

title: One New Englander is in this year’s class at Boston’s Folk Americana Roots Hall of Fame: Tom Rush
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Tom Rush, from Portsmouth, N.H., is the only New England artist in the 2026 Folk Americana Roots Hall of Fame class.
Rush will perform at the induction ceremony alongside Judy Collins and others at the Wang Theatre in Boston.
Other 2026 inductees include Neil Young, Jackson Browne, and Aretha Franklin, highlighting a mix of living and legacy artists.

title: Jaylen Brown sheds light on 1-on-1 meeting with Jayson Tatum ahead of playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown discussed a 1-on-1 meeting with Jayson Tatum before Tatum's return from a 10-month Achilles rehab.
The meeting focused on improving communication and setting the tone for the Boston Celtics ahead of the playoffs.
Brown emphasized the importance of his and Tatum's leadership amidst the Celtics' playoff push and external pressures.

title: Livestream: Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial gets underway
author: John Waller
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, was shot by a colleague last summer.
Fitzsimmons has pleaded not guilty to assault with a dangerous weapon charge.
Her trial is being livestreamed via NBC10 Boston as it begins this week.

title: How Dropkick Murphys’ Jeff DaRosa found himself on the road to the Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jeff DaRosa of Dropkick Murphys is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Claddagh Fund.
After quitting drinking at 40, DaRosa turned to running, finding discipline and structure offstage.
DaRosa's running journey included completing his first marathon in 2024 at the Mesa Marathon in Arizona.

title: Jayson Tatum, frustrated by shooting slump, is showing both rust and flashes
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum struggles with a shooting slump, hitting 38.8% from the field in recent games post-injury.
Tatum posted strong performances in other areas, averaging 8.9 rebounds per game since his return.
Despite challenges, Tatum feels progress and emphasizes taking a day-by-day approach after a lengthy rehab.

title: Bones Hyland, short-handed Timberwolves beat ice-cold Celtics: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Celtics lose to short-handed Timberwolves 102-92, struggling in the second half with a prolonged scoreless stretch.
Bones Hyland scored 23 points, leading Timberwolves offensively in the absence of Anthony Edwards, contributing critical plays.
Jayson Tatum struggled offensively, shooting 6-for-16, as he continues to return from rehabbing a significant injury.

title: Will President Trump send ICE agents to Boston’s Logan Airport?
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: President Trump plans to deploy ICE agents at airports due to the ongoing partial government shutdown.
Logan Airport in Boston has avoided long security lines despite TSA workers quitting over unpaid work.
TSA union in New England is unclear if ICE agents will be deployed, citing no official confirmation yet.
